subject: Project Management
title: Agile at the Pentagon
description: One-minute skill: Agile at the Pentagon.

hook.text: The U.S. Department of Defense once required waterfall for every project. Then it changed its mind.

buildup.text: The 2018 Defense Innovation Board recommended Agile for all software-intensive programs.

discovery.text: The Kessel Run team at the Air Force delivered combat software in weeks instead of years.

twist.text: Old procurement rules still demand detailed upfront specs — clashing directly with Agile principles.

climax.text: The Pentagon is Agile's toughest test: can iterative methods work inside rigid bureaucracy?

punchline.text: The institution that created waterfall is trying to escape it.

quiz.question: What is the Kessel Run team?
options: An Air Force Agile team delivering combat software rapidly
A SpaceX crew working with NASA
